18 surveys or final land plats for official approval or recording;
19 (5) otherwise engage in the practice of architecture (as defined in
20 IC 25-4-1);
21 (6) otherwise engage in the practice of professional engineering
22 (as defined in IC 25-31); IC 25-4-0.5-7);
23 (7) engage in the practice of surveying (as defined in IC 25-21.5);
24 or
25 (8) engage in the practice of professional geology (as defined in
26 IC 25-17.6).
27 (e) (b) This chapter, except section 10(a)(1) and 10(a)(2) of this
28 chapter, does not apply to:
29 (1) the practice of landscape architecture by any person who acts
30 under the supervision of a practitioner or by an employee of a
31 person lawfully engaged in the practice of landscape architecture
32 and who, in either event, does not assume responsible charge of
33 design or supervision;
34 (2) the practice of architecture or land planning and proper land
35 usage by a duly registered professional architect or the doing of
36 landscape architectural work by a registered architect or by an
37 employee under the supervision of a registered architect;
38 (3) the practice of engineering or land planning and proper land
39 usage by a duly registered professional engineer and the doing of
40 landscape architectural work by a registered professional engineer
41 or by an employee under supervision of a registered professional
42 engineer;
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

350
1 (4) the practice of surveying or land planning and proper land
2 usage by a professional surveyor and the doing of landscape
3 architectural work by a professional surveyor or by an employee
4 under supervision of a professional surveyor;
5 (5) the practice of landscape architecture by employees of the
6 United States government while engaged within this state in the
7 practice of landscape architecture for the United States
8 government;
9 (6) the practice of planning as is customarily done by regional,
10 park, or urban planners;
11 (7) the practice of arborists, foresters, gardeners, turf managers,
12 home builders, horticulturists, farmers, and other similar persons;
13 (8) the practice of any nurseryman or general or landscape
14 contractor, including design, planning, location, planting and
15 arrangements of plantings or other ornamental features; or
16 (9) the practice of natural resource professionals, including
17 biologists, geologists, or soil scientists.
18 SECTION 606. IC 25-4-3 IS ADDED TO THE INDIANA CODE
19 AS A NEW CHAPTER TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
20 JANUARY 1, 2027]:
21 Chapter 3. Regulation of Engineers
22 Sec. 1. The board shall enforce and administer the provisions of
23 this chapter, and adopt rules, not inconsistent with the Constitution
24 and laws of this state, as may be reasonably necessary for the
25 proper performance of its duties and the regulations of the
26 proceedings before it. The board shall adopt rules establishing
27 standards for the competent practice of engineering and for the
28 administration of the registered professional engineers and
29 registered engineering interns investigative fund established by
30 section 20 of this chapter. Any rulemaking by the board shall be in
31 accordance with IC 4-22-2.
32 Sec. 2. (a) Except as provided in IC 25-31-1-35 and subsection
33 (b), the licensing agency shall receive and account for all money
34 collected under the provisions of this chapter and shall deposit the
35 money with the treasurer of state to be deposited by the treasurer
36 of state in the general fund of the state.
37 (b) In addition to the registration fee established under section
38 13(c) of this chapter, the board shall establish a fee of not more
39 than twenty dollars ($20) for registered professional engineers and
40 registered engineering interns to be paid at the time of:
41 (1) issuance of a certificate of registration; and
42 (2) renewal of a certificate of registration;
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

351
1 under this chapter to provide funds for administering and
2 enforcing this chapter, including investigating and taking action
3 against persons violating this chapter. All funds collected under
4 this subsection shall be deposited into the registered professional
5 engineers and registered engineering interns investigative fund
6 established by section 20 of this chapter.
7 Sec. 3. (a) The following under either subdivision (1) or (2) shall
8 be considered as minimum evidence that the applicant is qualified
9 for registration as a professional engineer:
10 (1) All of the following:
11 (A) Graduation in an approved engineering curriculum of
12 four (4) years or more.
13 (B) A specific record of four (4) years or more of
14 progressive experience on engineering projects of sufficient
15 quality acquired subsequent to graduation, which
16 experience indicates that the applicant is qualified to be
17 placed in responsible charge of engineering work requiring
18 the exercise of judgment in the application of engineering
19 sciences to the sound solution of engineering problems.
20 (C) The successful passing of an examination as provided
21 for in section 5 of this chapter.
22 (2) All of the following:
23 (A) A specific record of eight (8) years or more of
24 engineering education and experience in engineering work,
25 which indicates that the applicant has acquired knowledge
26 and skill and practical experience in engineering work
27 approximating that required for registration as a
28 professional engineer under subdivision (1).
29 (B) The successful passing of an examination as provided
30 for in section 5 of this chapter.
31 (b) The following under either subdivision (1) or (2) shall be
32 considered as minimum evidence that the applicant is qualified for
33 certification as an engineering intern:
34 (1) All of the following:
35 (A) Graduation in an approved engineering curriculum of
36 four (4) years or more.
37 (B) The successful passing of an engineering intern
38 examination as provided in section 5 of this chapter.
39 (2) All of the following:
40 (A) A specific record of four (4) years or more of
41 engineering education and experience in engineering work
42 indicating that the applicant has acquired knowledge and
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

352
1 skill approximating that acquired through graduation in
2 an approved engineering curriculum of four (4) years or
3 more.
4 (B) The successful passing of an engineering intern
5 examination as provided in section 5 of this chapter.
6 (c) The board may waive the examination under section 5 of this
7 chapter to any applicant who has been granted a certificate of
8 registration under section 13 of this chapter.
9 (d) An individual is not eligible for registration as a professional
10 engineer or certification as an engineering intern if the individual
11 has been convicted of:
12 (1) an act which would constitute grounds for disciplinary
13 sanction under IC 25-1-11; or
14 (2) a felony that has a direct bearing on the individual's ability
15 to practice competently.
16 (e) In considering the qualifications of applicants, responsible
17 charge of engineering teaching shall be construed as responsible
18 charge of engineering work. An applicant who holds a degree of
19 master of science of engineering or the equivalent thereof from a
20 curriculum in engineering approved by the board may be given a
21 maximum credit of one (1) year of experience in addition to the
22 credit of four (4) years of education. An applicant who holds a
23 degree of doctor of philosophy or the equivalent thereof from a
24 curriculum in engineering approved by the board may be given a
25 maximum of credit of two (2) years of experience in addition to a
26 credit of four (4) years of education. Graduation in a course other
27 than engineering from a college or university acceptable to the
28 board may be considered as equivalent to two (2) years engineering
29 experience and education. The mere execution, as a contractor of
30 work designed by a professional engineer, or the supervision of the
31 construction of such work as a foreman or superintendent shall not
32 be deemed to be engineering experience.
33 (f) Any person having the necessary qualifications to be
34 registered under this chapter is eligible for registration although
35 the individual may not be engaged in the practice of engineering at
36 the time the application is made.
37 Sec. 4. (a) An individual applying for registration as a
38 professional engineer or for certification as an engineering intern
39 shall apply for registration or certification on a form prescribed
40 and provided by the board. Each application for registration shall
41 contain statements showing the education and qualifications of the
42 applicant and a detailed summary of the technical work performed
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

353
1 by the applicant. An application for registration as a professional
2 engineer shall be accompanied by the names of five (5) persons to
3 be used as references, at least three (3) of whom are professional
4 engineers who have a personal knowledge of the experience of the
5 applicant. Each application for a certification as an engineering
6 intern must contain statements showing the education of the
7 applicant, and except for an application for an examination to be
8 given during the applicant's senior year, the application shall be
9 accompanied by the names of three (3) persons to be used as
10 references. All applications shall be certified to by the applicant as
11 to the correctness of the statements contained in the application.
12 (b) A person who knowingly makes a false statement in the
13 application commits a Class A misdemeanor.
14 (c) The amount of registration fees for a professional engineer
15 and for an engineering intern shall be fixed and determined by the
16 board under IC 25-1-8-2. The board shall also in its rules prescribe
17 the manner and the time for the paying of registration fees.
18 (d) In the event the board refuses to issue a certificate of
19 registration to any person who has made proper application for
20 registration as a professional engineer, any initial fee prescribed by
21 the board and deposited with the board by an applicant shall be
22 retained by the board as an application fee.
23 (e) In the event the board refuses to issue a certificate to any
24 person who has made proper application for certification as an
25 engineering intern, the fee prescribed by the board and deposited
26 by the applicant with the board shall be retained as an application
27 fee.
28 Sec. 5. (a) The examination required of all applicants for
29 registration as a professional engineer shall be a written or
30 computer based examination which shall be divided into the
31 following two (2) parts:
32 (1) Engineering fundamentals.
33 (2) Principles and practice of engineering.
34 The board may adopt rules under IC 4-22-2 establishing additional
35 examination requirements.
36 (b) The engineering fundamentals portion of the examination
37 shall be designed to test the applicant's knowledge of mathematics
38 and the physical and engineering sciences. The standards of
39 proficiency required shall approximate that attained by graduation
40 in an approved four (4) year engineering curriculum.
41 (c) The principles and practice of the engineering portion of the
42 examination shall be designed primarily to test the applicant's
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

354
1 understanding of, and judgment and ability to apply correctly, the
2 principles of:
3 (1) mathematics;
4 (2) the physical sciences;
5 (3) the engineering sciences; and
6 (4) engineering design analysis and synthesis;
7 to the practice of professional engineering. A part of the
8 examination may be designed to test the applicant's knowledge and
9 understanding of the ethical, economic, and legal principles
10 relating to the practices of professional engineering.
11 (d) An applicant for registration as a professional engineer who
12 holds an engineering intern certificate issued in Indiana or in any
13 other state or territory having equivalent standards may be
14 exempted from the engineering fundamentals portion of the
15 examination.
16 (e) An applicant must successfully pass the engineering
17 fundamentals portion of the examination before taking the
18 principles and practice portion of the examination.
19 (f) Examinations shall be held at times and places as determined
20 by the board at least two (2) times each year. Examinations for
21 certification as an engineering intern may be held separately from
22 the examinations for registration as a professional engineer.
23 (g) An applicant for registration as a professional engineer who
24 is presently registered in another state or territory may be assigned
25 a written or computer based examination as the board deems
26 necessary to meet the requirements of this chapter.
27 (h) An applicant shall have three (3) attempts to pass each part
28 of the examination within a time period established by the board
29 by rule. The board may specify by rule the time required between
30 examinations and the opportunity for future examination attempts
31 for an applicant who fails to appear at an examination. The
32 amount of the fee to be paid for each examination shall be
33 determined by the board under IC 25-1-8-2.
34 (i) If an applicant who has failed a part of the examination three
35 (3) times reapplies and submits evidence of acquiring additional
36 knowledge for the examination, the board may give the applicant
37 approval to take subsequent examinations or partial examinations.
38 Sec. 6. (a) The board shall issue a certificate of registration,
39 upon the payment of the fee prescribed by the board under
40 IC 25-1-8-2 and the fee established by section 2(b) of this chapter,
41 to any applicant who, in the opinion of the board, has satisfactorily
42 met all requirements of this chapter. In the case of a professional
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

355
1 engineer, the certificate shall authorize the practice of
2 "professional engineering". The certificate of registration shall:
3 (1) show the full name of the registrant;
4 (2) bear a serial number and date; and
5 (3) be signed by a designee of the board.
6 The issuance of any certificate of registration by the board under
7 this chapter is evidence that the individual named on the certificate
8 is entitled to all the rights and privileges of a registered
9 professional engineer from the date on the certificate until it
10 expires or is revoked.
11 (b) The board shall issue a certificate of enrollment upon the
12 payment of the certificate fee prescribed by the board under
13 IC 25-1-8-2 to any applicant who, in the opinion of the board, has
14 satisfactorily met all of the requirements of this chapter. In the
15 case of an engineering intern, the certificate shall state that the
16 applicant has successfully passed the examination in engineering
17 fundamentals and has been enrolled as an engineering intern. The
18 certificate of enrollment shall:
19 (1) show the full name of the enrollee;
20 (2) bear a serial number and date; and
21 (3) be signed by the director of the licensing agency.
22 The issuance of a certificate of enrollment by the board is evidence
23 that the individual named on the certificate is entitled to all the
24 rights and privileges of an engineering intern while the certificate
25 remains unrevoked or until it expires.
26 Sec. 7. (a) The granting of registration extends to the registrant
27 the authority to use a seal of a design approved by the board
28 bearing the registrant's name, registration number, and the legend
29 "professional engineer".
30 (b) During the period of time that a registrant's certificate is
31 valid, the registrant is authorized to apply the registrant's seal to
32 plans, specifications, studies, drawings, and reports. Applying the
33 registrant's seal attests that:
34 (1) the work embodies the engineering work of the registrant;
35 (2) the registrant or an employed subordinate supervised by
36 the registrant prepared the documents, and in the context of
37 engineered plans "prepared" refers to the registrant's control
38 and direction of the engineering work and design process;
39 (3) the registrant assumes full professional responsibility for
40 the documents; and
41 (4) the work meets standards of acceptable engineering
42 practice.
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

356
1 (c) It is unlawful for any person to stamp or seal any document
2 with a seal after the certificate of the registrant named on the seal
3 has expired or has been revoked.
4 Sec. 8. (a) Subject to IC 25-1-2-6(e), unless renewed, a certificate

10 before the renewal date specified by the licensing agency.
11 (b) Subject to IC 25-1-2-6(e), if the holder of a certificate does

13 agency, the certificate expires and becomes invalid without the
14 board taking any action.
15 (c) The failure on the part of a registrant to renew a certificate

25 Sec. 9. (a) The board may adopt rules requiring a professional

27 under section 8 of this chapter.

35 (2) Establish requirements for reactivation of an inactive
36 certificate.
37 Sec. 10. (a) A registration certificate for a professional engineer
38 may be issued only to a natural person.
39 (b) A business, including a proprietorship, partnership, or
40 corporation, doing business in Indiana may not practice or offer to
41 practice engineering unless that practice is carried on under the
42 responsible direction and supervision of a registered professional
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

357
1 engineer who is a full-time employee of the business. All plans,
2 sheets of designs, specifications, reports, studies, or other
3 engineering documents that require certification and are prepared
4 by the personnel of a business must carry the signature and seal of
5 the registered professional engineer who is in responsible charge
6 of the professional engineering work.
7 Sec. 11. (a) A county, city, town, township, school corporation,
8 or other political subdivision of this state may not engage in the
9 construction or maintenance of any public work involving the
10 practice of engineering for which plans, specifications, and
11 estimates have not been prepared, certified, and sealed by, and the
12 construction and maintenance executed under the direct
13 supervision of, a professional engineer. Any contract executed in
14 violation of this section is void.
15 (b) An official of this state, or of any city, town, county,
16 township, or school corporation, charged with the enforcement of
17 any law, ordinance, or rule relating to the design, construction, or
18 alteration of buildings or structures may not use or accept or
19 approve any plans or specifications that have not been prepared
20 by, or under the supervision of and certified by, a registered
21 professional engineer. This subsection does not apply:
22 (1) to plans or specifications prepared by, or under the
23 supervision of and certified by, an architect who is registered
24 under IC 25-4-1;
25 (2) to structures and construction listed in IC 22-15-3-3(a); or
26 (3) to plans or specifications contained in a registration,
27 license, or permit application, including an application for an
28 initial permit, the renewal of a permit, the modification of a
29 permit, or a variance from a permit submitted to the
30 commissioner of the department of environmental
31 management under IC 13, unless the permit is for the
32 approval of plans or specifications for construction for which
33 a professional engineer's seal is required by operation of
34 either state or federal law, rule, or regulation. This subsection
35 does not require a professional engineer's seal for an
36 application for an air quality construction permit under 326
37 IAC 2-1-3.
38 This section shall not be construed as to abridge or otherwise affect
39 the powers of any state board or department to issue rules
40 governing the safety of buildings or structures.
41 (c) All maps required to show the underground workings of any
42 mine in Indiana must be prepared, certified, and sealed by a
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

358
1 professional engineer or professional surveyor.
2 Sec. 12. (a) An employee or a subordinate of any person who
3 holds a certificate of registration under the provisions of this
4 chapter is exempt from the provisions of this chapter if the practice
5 of the employee or subordinate does not include responsible charge
6 of design or supervision.
7 (b) This chapter does not require registration for the purpose of
8 practicing engineering by an individual or a business:
9 (1) on property owned or leased by that individual or business
10 unless the engineering practice involves the public health or
11 safety, or the health or safety of the employees of that
12 individual or business;
13 (2) for the performance of engineering which relates solely to
14 the design or fabrication of manufactured products; or
15 (3) that is registered as a landscape architect under IC 25-4-2
16 and while the individual or business is engaged in the practice
17 of landscape architecture planning the use of land or water.
18 Sec. 13. The board may, upon application and payment of a fee
19 established by the board in the board's rules, issue a certificate of
20 registration as a professional engineer to an individual who holds
21 a valid certificate of registration as a professional engineer, issued
22 to the applicant by the proper authority of any state or territory or
23 possession of the United States if the requirements for registration
24 of professional engineers that the certificate of registration was
25 issued under do not conflict with the provisions of this chapter. In
26 determining the qualifications of an applicant, the board may
27 accept the verified professional record of the applicant that is
28 certified by the National Council of Examiners for Engineers and
29 Surveyors. However, an applicant meets the experience
30 requirement under section 3 of this chapter if the applicant:
31 (1) has at least three (3) years of engineering work experience
32 after the applicant graduates from an approved engineering
33 curriculum but before the applicant successfully passes an
34 examination required under section 5 of this chapter; and
35 (2) has been registered or licensed as a professional engineer
36 in another state for at least ten (10) years.
37 Sec. 14. A certificate of registration, or a certification as an
38 engineering intern to replace a certificate which has been lost,
39 destroyed, or mutilated, may be issued subject to the rules of the
40 board and the fee established under IC 25-1-8-2.
41 Sec. 15. A person who:
42 (1) practices or offers to practice engineering without being
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

359
1 registered or exempted under the laws of this state;
2 (2) presents as the person's own the certificate of registration
3 or the seal of another;
4 (3) gives any false or forged evidence of any kind to the board
5 or to any member of the board in obtaining a certificate of
6 registration;
7 (4) impersonates any other registrant;
8 (5) uses an expired, suspended, or revoked certificate of
9 registration; or
10 (6) otherwise violates this chapter;
11 commits a Class B misdemeanor.
12 Sec. 16. (a) It is the duty of all law enforcement officers of this
13 state, or any political subdivision, to enforce the provisions of this
14 chapter and to apprehend and prosecute any person who violates
15 any of the provisions of this chapter.
16 (b) The attorney general shall act as the legal advisor of the
17 board and render any legal assistance as may be necessary in
18 carrying out the provisions of this chapter.
19 (c) The attorney general and the licensing agency may use the
20 registered professional engineers and registered engineering
21 interns investigative fund established by section 20 of this chapter
22 to hire investigators and other employees to enforce the provisions
23 of this chapter and to investigate and prosecute violations of this
24 chapter.
25 Sec. 17. (a) The attorney general, the prosecuting attorney of
26 any county, the board, or a citizen of any county where a person
27 who is not exempted engages in the practice of engineering without
28 first having obtained a certificate of registration or without first
29 having renewed an expired certificate of registration may, in
30 accordance with the provisions of the laws of this state governing
31 injunctions, file an action in the name of the state of Indiana to
32 enjoin that person from engaging in the practice of engineering
33 until a certificate of registration is secured or renewed.
34 (b) Any person who has been enjoined and violates an injunction
35 shall be punished for contempt of court. An injunction does not
36 relieve a person engaged in the practice of engineering without a
37 certificate of registration or without first having renewed an
38 expired certificate of registration from a criminal prosecution.
39 (c) The remedy by injunction is in addition to any remedy
40 provided for herein for the criminal prosecution of the offender. In
41 charging any person in a complaint for violation of the provisions
42 of this chapter by engaging in the practice of engineering without
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

360
1 a certificate of registration or without having renewed an expired
2 certificate of registration, it is sufficient to charge that the
3 offender:
4 (1) on a certain day in a certain county practiced or offered to
5 practice engineering; and
6 (2) was not registered or exempted under this chapter.
7 Sec. 18. (a) Except as provided in IC 25-4-1-11, a person
8 registered as a professional engineer under this chapter is exempt
9 from the provisions of any and all statutes in force in this state
10 regulating the practice of architecture.
11 (b) This chapter does not apply to an individual registered as an
12 architect under IC 25-4-1. An architect, however, who is registered

15 is registered under this chapter.
16 Sec. 19. (a) This section does not apply to a job position filled by
17 the department of environmental management.
18 (b) Unless a job position is filled by a professional engineer, the
19 state or a political subdivision (as defined in IC 36-1-2-13) may not
20 use the word "engineer" in the position's job title.
21 Sec. 20. (a) The registered professional engineers and registered
22 engineering interns investigative fund is established to provide
23 funds for administering and enforcing the provisions of this
24 chapter, including investigating and taking enforcement action
25 against violators of this chapter. The fund shall be administered by
26 the attorney general and the licensing agency.
27 (b) The expenses of administering the fund shall be paid from
28 the money in the fund. The fund consists of money from the fee
29 imposed upon registered professional engineers and registered
30 engineering interns under section 9(b) of this chapter.
31 (c) The treasurer of state shall invest the money in the fund not
32 currently needed to meet the obligations of the fund in the same
33 manner as other public money may be invested.
34 (d) Money in the fund at the end of a state fiscal year does not
35 revert to the state general fund. If the total amount in the fund
36 exceeds five hundred thousand dollars ($500,000) at the end of a
37 state fiscal year after payment of all claims and expenses, the
38 amount that exceeds five hundred thousand dollars ($500,000)
39 reverts to the state general fund.
40 (e) Money in the fund is continually appropriated for use by the
41 attorney general and the licensing agency to administer and
42 enforce the provisions of this chapter and to conduct investigations
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

361
1 and take enforcement action against persons violating the
2 provisions of this chapter.

9 SECTION 607. IC 25-17.6-7-1 IS AMENDED TO READ AS
10 FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE JANUARY 1, 2027]: Sec. 1. The following
11 persons are exempt from the requirements of licensure under this
12 article:
13 (1) An officer or employee of the United States government, state
14 government, or local government while engaged in providing
15 geological services for the officer's or employee's employers.
16 (2) A person engaged solely in geological research or the
17 instruction of geology.
18 (3) A person not engaged in the public practice of geology.
19 (4) A professional engineer registered under IC 25-31 IC 25-4-3
20 who applies geology to the practice of engineering.
21 (5) A soil scientist who is certified as a soil classifier or soil
22 scientist by the American Registry of Certified Professionals in
23 Agronomy, Crops and Soils (ARCPACS) and the Indiana
24 Association of Professional Soil Classifiers (IAPSC) and who
25 applies geology in the practice of soil science.

362
1 chairperson of the committee is not allowed to cast a vote on any

14 until a successor is appointed.
15 (f) On or before July 1, 2027, and July 1 biennially thereafter, the
16 committee shall submit a report to the executive director of the
17 legislative services agency, in an electronic format under IC 5-14-6, for
18 review by the interim committee on government in accordance with
19 IC 1-1-15.5-4 and IC 2-5-1.3-13(g). The report shall describe:
20 (1) official action taken; and
21 (2) actionable items considered;
22 by the committee during the preceding two (2) years.

35 medical services" has the meaning set forth in IC 16-18-2-110.
36 (b) As used in this section, "emergency medical services provider
37 organization" means a provider of emergency medical services that is
38 certified by the Indiana emergency medical services fire and EMS
39 standards and education commission as an advanced life support
40 provider organization under rules adopted under IC 16-31-3.
41 (c) As used in this section, "policy of accident and sickness
42 insurance" has the meaning set forth in IC 27-8-5-1. However, for
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

388
1 purposes of this section, the term does not include the following:
2 (1) Accident only, credit, dental, vision, Medicare supplement,
3 long term care, or disability income insurance.
4 (2) Coverage issued as a supplement to liability insurance.
5 (3) Automobile medical payment insurance.
6 (4) A specified disease policy.
7 (5) A policy that provides a stipulated daily, weekly, or monthly
8 payment to an insured without regard to the actual expense of the
9 confinement.
10 (6) A short term insurance plan (as defined in IC 27-8-5.9-3).
11 (d) A policy of accident and sickness insurance that provides
12 coverage for emergency medical services must provide reimbursement
13 for emergency medical services that are:
14 (1) rendered by an emergency medical services provider
15 organization;
16 (2) within the emergency medical services provider organization's
17 scope of practice;
18 (3) performed or provided as advanced life support services; and
19 (4) performed or provided:
20 (A) during a response initiated through the 911 system; or
21 (B) as part of a mobile integrated healthcare program
22 described in IC 16-31-12;
23 regardless of whether the patient is transported.
24 (e) Reimbursement for basic and advanced life support services
25 through a policy to which this section applies must be provided on an
26 equal basis regardless of whether the services involve transportation of
27 the patient by ambulance.
28 (f) If multiple emergency medical services provider organizations
29 qualify and submit a claim for reimbursement under this section for an
30 encounter, the insurer:
31 (1) may reimburse under this section only for one (1) claim per
32 patient encounter; and
33 (2) shall reimburse the claim submitted by the emergency medical
34 services provider organization that performed or provided the
35 majority of advanced life support services for the patient.
36 (g) The department may adopt rules under IC 4-22-2 to implement
37 this section.
38 (h) This section does not require a policy of accident and sickness
39 insurance to provide coverage for emergency medical services.

26 from these investments shall be deposited in the fund.

28 be allotted and allocated at the beginning of the fiscal period for which
29 the appropriation was made.
30 (g) Money in the fund at the end of a state fiscal year does not revert
31 to the state general fund or any other fund.

33 department all money in the fund for the purposes of this chapter.

42 SECTION 668. IC 31-27-2-8, AS AMENDED BY P.L.183-2017,
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

399
1 SECTION 13, IS AMENDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
2 JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 8. (a) Except as provided in subsection (f), the
3 department may grant a variance or waiver of a rule governing child
4 caring institutions, foster family homes, group homes, or child placing
5 agencies. A variance or waiver granted under this section must promote
6 statewide practices and must protect the rights of persons affected by
7 this article.
8 (b) The department may grant a variance to a rule if an applicant for
9 a license or a licensee under this article does the following:
10 (1) Submits to the department a written request for the variance
11 in the form and manner specified by the department.
12 (2) Documents that compliance with an alternative method of
13 compliance approved by the department will not be adverse to the
14 health, safety, or welfare of a child receiving services from the
15 applicant for the variance, as determined by the department.
16 (c) A variance granted under subsection (b) must be conditioned
17 upon compliance with the alternative method approved by the
18 department. Noncompliance constitutes the violation of a rule of the
19 department and may be the basis for revoking the variance.
20 (d) The department may grant a waiver of a rule if an applicant for
21 a license or a licensee under this article does the following:
22 (1) Submits to the department a written request for the waiver in
23 the form and manner specified by the department.
24 (2) Documents that compliance with the rule specified in the
25 application for the waiver will create an undue hardship on the
26 applicant for the waiver, as determined by the department.
27 (3) Documents that the applicant for the waiver will be in
28 substantial compliance with the rules adopted by the department
29 after the waiver is granted, as determined by the department.
30 (4) Documents that noncompliance with the rule specified in the
31 application for a waiver will not be adverse to the health, safety,
32 or welfare of a child receiving services from the applicant for the
33 waiver, as determined by the department.
34 (e) Except for a variance or waiver of a rule governing foster family
35 homes, a variance or waiver of a rule under this section that conflicts
36 with a building rule or fire safety rule adopted by the fire prevention
37 and building safety commission department of homeland security is
38 not effective until the variance or waiver is approved by the fire
39 prevention and building safety commission. department of homeland
40 security.

4 SECTION 673. IC 32-21-2.5-9 IS REPEALED [EFFECTIVE JULY
5 1, 2026]. Sec. 9. (a) The electronic recording commission is established
6 to adopt standards to implement this chapter before January 1, 2018.
7 The commission consists of the following five (5) members appointed
8 by the governor:
9 (1) Three (3) members must be county recorders.
10 (2) One (1) member must be employed in Indiana in the banking
11 or mortgage lending industry.
12 (3) One (1) member must be employed in Indiana in the land title
13 industry.
14 (b) To keep the standards and practices of county recorders in
15 Indiana in harmony with the standards and practices of recording
16 offices in other jurisdictions that enact substantially this chapter and to
17 keep the technology used by county recorders in Indiana compatible
18 with technology used by recording offices in other jurisdictions that
19 enact substantially this chapter, the electronic recording commission,
20 so far as is consistent with the purposes, policies, and provisions of this
21 chapter, in adopting, amending, and repealing standards shall consider:
22 (1) standards and practices of other jurisdictions;
23 (2) the most recent standards promulgated by national standard
24 setting bodies, such as the Property Records Industry Association
25 (PRIA);
26 (3) the views of interested persons and governmental officials and
27 entities;
28 (4) the needs of counties of varying size, population, and
29 resources; and
30 (5) standards requiring adequate information security protection
31 to ensure that electronic documents are accurate, authentic,
32 adequately preserved, and resistant to tampering.

11 FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE JANUARY 1, 2027]: Sec. 13. (a) A person

17 supervision of the county surveyor or, if the county surveyor is not
18 registered under IC 25-21.5 or IC 25-31, IC 25-4-3, the registered
19 person who is selected under section 11 of this chapter. The surveyor

22 of any monument. A person who wishes to have the location of a
23 monument changed must make a request to the surveyor in writing and
24 furnish written approval of all landowners whose property is affected
25 by the proposed change. The surveyor may approve, reject, or modify
26 the request and shall file a copy of the notice and the landowners'
27 consents in the corner record book.

29 municipal road or street, it is necessary to remove or bury a monument
30 marking a corner, the owner of the public right-of-way shall notify the
31 county surveyor in writing at least fifteen (15) days before commencing
32 the work.

34 (1) prohibiting a person from moving, changing, or otherwise
35 altering a monument marking a corner without complying with
36 this section; and

38 ordinance.
39 Any money collected shall be deposited in the county surveyor's corner

25 SECTION 712. IC 36-8-2.5-1, AS AMENDED BY P.L.7-2021,
26 SECTION 2, IS AMENDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
27 JANUARY 1, 2027]: Sec. 1. The following definitions apply
28 throughout this chapter:
29 (1) "Communication" means an oral or written statement, note,
30 record, recording, report, or document made during, or arising out
31 of, a first responder's receipt of CISM services or peer support
32 services.
33 (2) "Critical incident" means an actual or perceived event or
34 situation that involves crisis, disaster, trauma, or emergency.
35 (3) "Critical incident stress" means the acute or cumulative
36 psychological stress or trauma that a first responder may
37 experience in providing emergency services in response to a
38 critical incident. The stress or trauma is an unusually strong
39 emotional, cognitive, behavioral, or physical reaction that may
40 interfere with normal functioning by causing:
41 (A) physical and emotional injury or illness;
42 (B) failure of a usual coping mechanism;
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

418
1 (C) loss of interest in work or normal life activities;
2 (D) loss of ability to function; or
3 (E) psychological disruption of personal life, including the
4 emergency responder's relationship with a spouse, child,
5 relative, or friend.
6 (4) "CISM services" means critical incident stress management
7 services that include programs and services related to education,
8 prevention, and mitigation of the effects from exposure to highly
9 stressful critical incidents. The term includes services provided by
10 an individual certified to provide group crisis intervention,
11 individual crisis intervention, peer support, or any related service.
12 (5) "CISM services provider" means an individual who is certified
13 to provide CISM services and who is acting as part of a CISM
14 team or in another official capacity.
15 (6) "CISM team" means an organized community or local crisis
16 response team that is trained and certified under standards
17 substantially similar to the training and certification standards
18 established by the Indiana emergency medical services fire and
19 EMS standards and education commission as set forth under
20 IC 16-31-2.
21 (7) "First responder" has the meaning set forth in IC 10-10.5-2-1.
22 (8) "First responder recipient" means an individual who is a first
23 responder and receives CISM services, peer support services, or
24 both.
25 (9) "Peer support services" means services provided by a person
26 who:
27 (A) is a first responder working for the same department or
28 public agency that the first responder recipient works for; and
29 (B) acts:
30 (i) in the person's official capacity as a peer support team
31 member; and
32 (ii) within the written peer support guidelines that are in
33 effect for the person's respective first responder agency
34 program.
35 The term includes active listening, assessment, mentoring, crisis
36 intervention, or other support services provided to an employee
37 experiencing personal or professional difficulties that may or may
38 not be a result of the employee's exposure to highly stressful
39 critical incidents.
40 (10) "Peer support team member" means a first responder who:
41 (A) has successfully completed peer support training; and
42 (B) is designated a peer support team member;
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

419
1 by the head of the first responder's agency or the president of the
2 bargaining unit of the employees of the department or agency.

21 orders given to the individuals by the chief of the volunteer fire
22 department or an officer of the volunteer fire department,
23 including orders or duties involving education and training.
24 "Employee" means a person in the service of another person under
25 a written or implied contract of hire or apprenticeship.
26 "Employer" means:
27 (1) a political subdivision;
28 (2) an individual or the legal representative of a deceased
29 individual;
30 (3) a firm;
31 (4) an association;
32 (5) a limited liability company;
33 (6) an employer that provides on-the-job training under the
34 federal School to Work Opportunities Act (20 U.S.C. 6101 et
35 seq.) to the extent set forth in IC 22-3-2-2.5(a); or
36 (7) a corporation or its receiver or trustee;
37 that uses the services of another person for pay.
38 "Essential employee" means an employee:
39 (1) who the employer has determined to be essential to the
40 operation of the employer's daily enterprise; and
41 (2) without whom the employer is likely to suffer economic injury
42 as a result of the absence of the essential employee.
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

425
1 "Nominal compensation" means annual compensation of not more
2 than twenty thousand dollars ($20,000).
3 "Public servant" has the meaning set forth in IC 35-31.5-2-261.
4 "Responsible party" has the meaning set forth in IC 13-11-2-191(d).
5 "Volunteer fire department" means a department or association
6 organized for the purpose of answering fire alarms, extinguishing fires,
7 and providing other emergency services, the majority of members of
8 which receive no compensation or nominal compensation for their
9 services.
10 "Volunteer firefighter" means a firefighter:
11 (1) who, as a result of a written application, has been elected or
12 appointed to membership in a volunteer fire department;
13 (2) who has executed a pledge to faithfully perform, with or
14 without nominal compensation, the work related duties assigned
15 and orders given to the firefighter by the chief of the volunteer
16 fire department or an officer of the volunteer fire department,
17 including orders or duties involving education and training as
18 prescribed by the volunteer fire department or the state; and
19 (3) whose name has been entered on a roster of volunteer
20 firefighters that is kept by the volunteer fire department and that
21 has been approved by the proper officers of the unit.
22 "Volunteer member" means a member of a volunteer emergency
23 medical services association connected with a unit as set forth in
24 IC 16-31-5-1(6).

19 SECTION 730. IC 36-9-27-71 IS AMENDED TO READ AS
20 FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE JANUARY 1, 2027]: Sec. 71. (a) When, in
21 the construction or reconstruction of a regulated drain, the county
22 surveyor determines that the proposed drain will cross a public
23 highway or the right-of-way of a railroad company at a point where:
24 (1) there is no crossing; or
25 (2) the crossing will not adequately handle or will be endangered
26 by the flow of water from the drain when completed;
27 the county surveyor shall include in the plans the grade and cross
28 section requirements for a new crossing, or the requirements for
29 altering, enlarging, repairing, or replacing the crossing. The surveyor
30 shall mail a copy of the requirements addressed to the owner of the
31 highway or right-of-way.
32 (b) When requested by the owner of the highway or right-of-way,
33 the county surveyor shall meet with the owner at a time and place to be
34 fixed by the surveyor. The surveyor shall hear objections to the
35 requirements, and may then change the requirements as justice may
36 require.
37 (c) When the board finds that in the construction, reconstruction, or
38 maintenance of a regulated drain it is necessary to:
39 (1) alter, enlarge, repair, or replace a crossing; or
40 (2) construct a new crossing where none existed before;
41 the cost of the work on the crossing shall be paid by the owner of the
42 public highway. This cost may not be considered by the county
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

432
1 surveyor or by the board in determining the cost of the work on the
2 drain or in assessing benefits and damages. However, if it is necessary
3 for the owner of a public highway to construct a new crossing because
4 of a cut-off for the purpose of shortening or straightening a regulated
5 drain, the owner of the public highway shall pay one-half (1/2) of the
6 cost of the new crossing, and the remainder shall be included in the
7 cost of the work on the drain.
8 (d) A railroad company with a right-of-way that is:
9 (1) crossed by the construction of a regulated drain; or
10 (2) affected by the altering or enlarging of a crossing;
11 shall pay one-half (1/2) of the cost of the work on the crossing and the
12 remainder shall be included in the cost of the work on the drain.
13 (e) If the county surveyor is registered under IC 25-31, IC 25-4-3,
14 the county surveyor must review and approve or disapprove the plans
15 and hydraulic data for an existing crossing that is to be altered,

COMMITTEE REPORT
Mr. Speaker: Your Committee on Ways and Means, to which was
referred House Bill 1003, has had the same under consideration and
begs leave to report the same back to the House with the
recommendation that said bill be amended as follows:
Page 7, between lines 33 and 34, begin a new paragraph and insert:
"SECTION 3. IC 2-5-53.5 IS ADDED TO THE INDIANA CODE
AS A NEW CHAPTER TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
UPON PASSAGE]:
Chapter 53.5. Government Reform Task Force
Sec. 1. As used in this chapter, "agency" has the meaning set
forth in IC 4-22-2-3.
Sec. 2. As used in this chapter, "council" refers to the legislative
council established by IC 2-5-1.1-1.
Sec. 3. As used in this chapter, "rule" has the meaning set forth
in IC 4-22-2-3.
Sec. 4. As used in this chapter, "task force" refers to the
government reform task force established by section 5 of this
chapter.
Sec. 5. The government reform task force is established.
Sec. 6. (a) Except as provided in subsections (b), (c), (d), and (e),
IC 2-5-1.2 applies to the task force.
(b) The task force consists of the following ten (10) members of
the general assembly:
(1) Three (3) members appointed by the president pro
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

446
tempore of the senate.
(2) Two (2) members appointed by the minority leader of the
senate.
(3) Three (3) members appointed by the speaker of the house
of representatives.
(4) Two (2) members appointed by the minority leader of the
house of representatives.
A member of the task force serves at the pleasure of the appointing
authority.
(c) The task force shall meet as called by the chair. All meetings
of the task force shall be open to the public in accordance with and
subject to IC 5-14-1.5. All records of the task force shall be subject
to the requirements of IC 5-14-3.
(d) A majority of the members of the task force constitutes a
quorum. The affirmative vote of at least a majority of the members
at a meeting at which a quorum is present is necessary for the task
force to take official action other than to meet and take testimony.
(e) The:
(1) chairman of the council shall designate the chair; and
(2) vice chairman of the council shall designate the vice chair;
of the task force from the members of the task force. The chair and
vice chair of the task force serve as chair and vice chair at the
pleasure of the appointing authority.
Sec. 7. The task force shall study:
(1) the feasibility of transferring the administration of the
office of the state chemist from Purdue University to the
Indiana state department of agriculture;
(2) any employment issue arising from a transfer of the
administration of the office of the state chemist; and
(3) any issue arising from a transfer of any property, records,
funds, or accounts of the office of state chemist to the Indiana
state department of agriculture.
Sec. 8. The task force may request information or testimony
from department or agency heads, or the designee of a department
or agency head, as determined necessary by the task force.
Sec. 9. The task force shall:
(1) develop recommendations in a report concerning the
issues set forth in section 7 of this chapter; and
(2) submit the report to the council in an electronic format
under IC 5-14-6 not later than November 1, 2026.
Sec. 10. The legislative services agency shall provide support
staff to the task force.
HB 1003—LS 6756/DI 92

447
Sec. 11. This chapter expires June 30, 2027.".
Page 10, delete lines 29 through 42.

Page 64, line 8, after "the" insert "Indiana".
Page 110, between lines 2 and 3, begin a new paragraph and insert:
"SECTION 195. IC 14-21-1-22.5 IS ADDED TO THE INDIANA
CODE AS A NEW SECTION TO READ AS FOLLOWS
[EFFECTIVE JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 22.5. (a) The review board shall
establish the Indiana historical marker program for marking
historical sites in Indiana. As a part of this program, the review
board shall fix a state format for historical markers. No person
may erect a historical marker in the state format without the
approval of the review board. All historical markers in the state
format shall be provided by the review board using appropriated
funds, local matching funds, donations, grants, or any other funds
provided for that purpose according to the guidelines and rules of
the historical marker program.
(b) The review board may appoint a historical marker advisory
committee to serve without compensation. The committee may
advise the review board and the director concerning the following:
(1) Guidelines and rules for the historical marker program.
(2) Appropriate sites to be marked.
(3) Other matters concerning the historical marker program
as requested by the review board or the director.
(c) Historical markers approved under this section, including
state format markers installed after 1945 and markers installed by
the Indiana Civil War centennial commission, are the property of
the state. Maintenance of state historical markers is part of the
historical marker program. The review board may cooperate with
individuals, local and state agencies, and private institutions and
organizations for the maintenance of the historical markers. Funds
made available to the historical marker program, as approved by
the review board, may be used for necessary maintenance.
(d) No historical marker may be erected on a highway of the
state highway system without the approval of the review board as
to its historical accuracy. This provision is in addition to any other
requirement of law.".

follows:

the house of representatives.
(2) Two (2) members of the senate appointed as follows:
(A) One (1) member appointed by the president pro
tempore of the senate.
(B) One (1) member appointed by the minority leader of
the senate.".
